TITLE: fix: Eliminate silent exception swallowing; document error-handling convention
LABELS: roadmap, type:fix, size:M, phase:A-foundation
---
## Context

Several `except Exception:` / bare `except:` blocks in `src/` swallow errors with a plain `pass` — no log line, no dialog, no comment. When one of them hides a real failure (a CUDA cleanup error, a broken log sink, a corrupt DICOM), the app degrades silently and the bug report contains nothing to work with. `docs/11_risks_and_technical_debt.md` documents the wider problem as the "Inconsistent Error Handling" debt item (~line 137): a mix of raised exceptions, message boxes, and silent `return None`.

Quality goal served: **reliability / operability** — every swallowed exception becomes either visible (logged) or surfaced (dialog), and a written convention prevents new silent sites.

Two concrete product fixes ride along:
1. A **CUDA/RAM out-of-memory on SAM model load** currently produces a generic error dialog; docs/11 "SAM 2 Large Model Crashes" (~line 25) asks for a friendly, actionable message (use a smaller model).
2. The single **bare `except:`** in the codebase (`dialogs/dicom_converter.py:171`) also swallows `KeyboardInterrupt`/`SystemExit`.

## Current state

All silent sites, verified by reading the code (line numbers current as of this issue; re-verify after the logging migration lands, they may shift slightly):

| # | Site | What it hides | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/inference/sam_utils.py:365-366` — `_log_device()` `except Exception: pass` | torch import / device-query failure while printing which device SAM uses | benign, but must be visible at debug |
| 2 | `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/inference/sam_utils.py:404-405` — `unload()` CUDA cache cleanup `except Exception: pass` | `torch.cuda.synchronize/empty_cache/ipc_collect` failure → GPU memory silently not reclaimed | user thinks unload worked |
| 3 | `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/inference/dino_utils.py:135-136` — `unload()` CUDA cache cleanup `except Exception: pass` | same as #2 for DINO | |
| 4 | `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/training/sam_trainer.py:245-246` — `_device_label()` `except Exception: pass` | GPU-name lookup failure; falls back to bare `str(device)` | benign fallback, log at debug |
| 5 | `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/training/mlflow_tracker.py:158-159` — `_emit()` `except Exception: pass` | the training-log sink (a Qt signal emit) itself raising → training log lines vanish | cannot re-emit through the broken sink; must use the module logger |
| 6 | `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/training/mlflow_tracker.py:202-203` — run-URL callback `except Exception: pass` | the MLflow-UI deep-link callback raising → no clickable link, no explanation | outer `except` at :204 already handles tracker failures; this inner one is only the callback |
| 7 | `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/dialogs/dicom_converter.py:171-172` — `apply_window_level()` **bare `except:`** + `pass` | VOI-LUT application failure → image shown without window/level, and bare `except:` also traps KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit | the only bare `except:` in `src/` |

Deliberate, **acceptable** narrow catches that stay as-is (they are the template for the convention): `controllers/yolo_controller.py:497-498` and `controllers/sam_train_controller.py:295-296` (`except TypeError: pass  # already disconnected`), `main.py:26-27` (`except ImportError: pass  # torch may not be installed…`).

OOM site: `SAMController.change_sam_model` (`src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/controllers/sam_controller.py:183-195`) catches all load errors generically and shows one message. `SAMUtils.change_sam_model` (`inference/sam_utils.py:325-344`) deliberately re-raises load-time exceptions ("network, corrupt weights, CUDA OOM"), so the controller is the right place to specialize.

## Proposed design

**Per-site fixes** (using `get_logger(__name__)` from `core/logging_config.py`, introduced by the logging issue):

- Sites 1, 4: `logger.debug("could not query torch device", exc_info=True)` (keep the existing fallback behavior).
- Sites 2, 3: `logger.warning("CUDA cache cleanup failed during unload; GPU memory may not be fully released", exc_info=True)`.
- Site 5: `logger.exception("training-log sink raised; message dropped: %s", msg)` — module logger, never the sink itself.
- Site 6: `logger.exception("MLflow run-URL callback raised; UI link not shown")`.
- Site 7: change to `except Exception:` + `logger.warning("DICOM window/level could not be applied; using raw pixel data", exc_info=True)`; keep `return image`.

**OOM-friendly SAM load dialog** — in `controllers/sam_controller.py:change_sam_model`, before the generic branch:

```python
def _is_oom(exc) -> bool:
    """True for CUDA or host out-of-memory errors, without importing torch."""
    if isinstance(exc, MemoryError):
        return True
    text = str(exc).lower()
    return isinstance(exc, RuntimeError) and (
        "out of memory" in text or "cuda oom" in text or "not enough memory" in text
    )
```

Put `_is_oom` in `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/core/torch_utils.py` (module-level function; torch-free so it is unit-testable anywhere). In the controller's `except Exception as e:` branch: if `_is_oom(e)`, show a tailored `QMessageBox.critical` — e.g. "Not enough GPU/system memory to load '<model>'. Close other applications or pick a smaller model (SAM 2 tiny or small are recommended)." — then reset the selector to index 0 exactly as today; otherwise keep the existing generic message. Both branches also `logger.exception(...)`. (`torch.cuda.OutOfMemoryError` is a `RuntimeError` subclass whose message contains "out of memory", so the string check covers it without a torch import.)

**Error-handling convention** (to be written into docs/08 and enforced in review):
1. Core / inference / io / training modules **raise**; they never show dialogs and never return `None` to signal failure of a user-initiated action.
2. Controllers / dialogs (the UI boundary) **catch**, `logger.exception(...)`, and surface via `QMessageBox` when a user action failed.
3. Catch the **narrowest** exception type that models the expected failure; `except Exception` is allowed only at a UI boundary or a documented crash-safety barrier (e.g. mlflow_tracker "never let tracking abort training").
4. **Never `pass` silently**: every swallowed exception must log (`exc_info=True`/`.exception`) or carry a narrow type plus a `# reason` comment (the `except TypeError: pass  # already disconnected` pattern).
5. Bare `except:` is banned.

## Implementation steps

1. Fix sites 1-7 per the table above (add the `logger = get_logger(__name__)` preamble where the logging migration hasn't already; `dialogs/dicom_converter.py` will have it).
2. Add `_is_oom` to `core/torch_utils.py`; wire the OOM branch into `SAMController.change_sam_model` with the tailored dialog; keep the selector-reset behavior.
3. Sweep for regressions of the pattern: run the grep checks in Acceptance criteria; confirm the only remaining `pass` catches are the three documented narrow ones.
4. Write tests (below).
5. Write the convention into docs/08 + the new ADR; update docs/11 (both the debt item and the SAM-Large risk mitigation).
6. `pytest tests/ -v` + smoke tests + headless app launch.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `grep -rn --include='*.py' -A1 'except Exception:' src/ | grep -E ':\s*pass\s*$'` returns **zero** matches.
- [ ] `grep -rnE --include='*.py' 'except\s*:' src/` returns **zero** matches (no bare `except:`).
- [ ] All seven enumerated sites log with `exc_info=True` or `.exception(...)`; sites 5-6 use the module logger, not the sink/callback.
- [ ] `_is_oom` exists in `core/torch_utils.py`, is importable without torch installed, and `change_sam_model` shows the smaller-model guidance on OOM while still resetting `sam_model_selector` to index 0.
- [ ] Non-OOM load failures keep the existing generic dialog text and selector reset (no regression).
- [ ] The `except TypeError: pass  # already disconnected` sites and `main.py`'s `except ImportError` are unchanged.
- [ ] Convention documented in docs/08; new ADR added; docs/11 updated.
- [ ] `pytest tests/ -v` and `pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v` pass.

## Test plan

- **Extend `tests/unit/test_torch_utils.py`** (exists):
  - `test_is_oom_memoryerror` — `MemoryError()` → True.
  - `test_is_oom_cuda_message` — `RuntimeError("CUDA out of memory. Tried to allocate …")` → True.
  - `test_is_oom_plain_runtimeerror` — `RuntimeError("weights corrupt")` → False; `ValueError("out of memory")` → False (wrong type).
- **Extend `tests/unit/test_mlflow_tracker.py`** (exists):
  - `test_emit_swallows_and_logs_broken_sink` — `set_log(sink)` where sink raises; `_emit("x")` does not raise; `caplog` (logger `digitalsreeni_image_annotator.training.mlflow_tracker`, propagate enabled for the test) contains the dropped message.
- **New `tests/integration/test_sam_model_load_errors.py`** — build a window with the fixture pattern from `tests/integration/test_sam_train_controller.py:12-18` (`ImageAnnotator()` + `deleteLater`, session `qt_application` fixture from `tests/conftest.py`):
  - `test_oom_load_shows_friendly_dialog` — monkeypatch `window.sam_utils.change_sam_model` to raise `RuntimeError("CUDA out of memory")`; capture `QMessageBox.critical` via `monkeypatch.setattr(QMessageBox, "critical", staticmethod(recorder))`; call `window.sam_controller.change_sam_model("SAM 2 large")`; assert the captured text mentions a smaller model and `window.sam_model_selector.currentIndex() == 0`.
  - `test_generic_load_failure_keeps_existing_dialog` — same with `RuntimeError("404 not found")`; assert generic wording and selector reset.
  - Never call the real `SAMUtils.change_sam_model` (it downloads 40-400 MB weights).

Commands: `pytest tests/unit/test_torch_utils.py tests/unit/test_mlflow_tracker.py tests/integration/test_sam_model_load_errors.py -v`, then `pytest tests/ -v`.

## Docs to update

- `docs/08_crosscutting_concepts.md` — add an "Error-Handling Convention" subsection under the existing "Error Handling" section (~line 391) with the five rules above plus the good/bad `except` examples.
- `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md` — new ADR (next free ADR number): "Raise in core, catch + dialog at the UI boundary, no silent pass" — context (docs/11 debt), decision (the five rules), consequences.
- `docs/11_risks_and_technical_debt.md` — update "Inconsistent Error Handling" (~line 137) to resolved-with-convention; add the OOM dialog as a mitigation under "SAM 2 Large Model Crashes" (~line 25).

## Pitfalls & guardrails

- **Blocked by the logging issue** — the fixes need `core/logging_config.py` / `get_logger`. Do not introduce `print()` (its AC forbids it).
- **ADR-013 (SAM in-flight guard)**: `SAMController.apply_sam_prediction` deliberately swallows `InferenceBusyError` (sam_controller.py:111-117) — that is a documented re-entrancy guard, **not** a silent-swallow site. Leave it. Same for the `_sam_inference_in_flight` flag set before `sam_utils.apply_sam_*` and cleared in `finally`.
- **mlflow_tracker's outer `except Exception` at :204** ("never let tracking abort training") is a documented crash-safety barrier and already reports via `_emit`; only the two inner silent sites change.
- Inference checklist (CLAUDE.md): when touching `sam_utils.py` / `dino_utils.py`, verify the model loads end-to-end if you have the hardware — no silent load failure, masks returned, UI responsive during inference. At minimum, verify the failure paths via the mocked tests above.
- Do not convert the deliberate narrow catches (`except TypeError: pass  # already disconnected`, `except ImportError` in main.py) — they are the convention's positive examples.
- Keep `unload()` behavior identical apart from logging: refs still dropped, `gc.collect()` still runs — the documented GPU-release recipe (model.cpu → gc.collect → empty_cache/ipc_collect/synchronize; full reclaim needs app restart) must not change.
- `caplog` tests: package loggers have `propagate = False` after `configure()`; either enable propagation on the specific logger for the test or attach `caplog.handler` — note this in the test to avoid a flaky green.

## Dependencies

Blocked by #33 (logging framework).

_Workflow: follow CLAUDE.md — feature branch, run smoke tests (`pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`), update arc42 docs, run the senior-reviewer agent before the PR._
